title: OperatorData
summary: Packages the weighted OR operator data and the Laplacian word attached to each OR index.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Packages the weighted OR operator data and the Laplacian word attached to each OR index.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/OR/OperatorData.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Concrete OR operator data with rational coefficient parameters. Its mathematical data are: N: the natural numbers w₁ : ℚ w₂ : ℚ; n: ℚ; coeff System: ORCoefficient System N.

Input weights cast to real numbers for the ambient calculus. In explicit terms, for d of type oroperator data, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.bi Weights D.w₁ D.w₂.
correspondence.leanType: def inputWeights (D : OROperatorData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : Fin 2 → ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Expected real output weight of an OR operator of total degree N. In explicit terms, for d of type oroperator data, its defining expression is D.w₁ plus D.w₂ minus 2 times D.N.

The rank-two ambient operator associated to the OR data. In explicit terms, for d of type oroperator data, and calconf of type calculus conf, its defining expression is Cal Conf.operator Sum ORIndex.support D.N the function sending I to D.coeff System.coeff R I the function sending I to I.to Word.to Operator Cal Conf.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (D : OROperatorData) (CalConf : Calculus Conf) : CalConf.BiOperator

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For D of type OROperator Data, F of type Cal Conf.Bi Input, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, D.to Operator Cal Conf F U equals ∑ I belongs to ORIndex.support D.N, D.coeff System.coeff I times I.to Word.to Operator Cal Conf F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,D\colon \operatorname{OROperatorData},\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{BiInput},\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad D.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F U = \sum I \in \operatorname{ORIndex}.\operatorname{support} N_{\mathrm{D}}, D.\operatorname{coeffSystem}.\operatorname{coeff} I \cdot I.\operatorname{toWord}.\operatorname{toOperator} \operatorname{CalConf} F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem toOperator_apply (D : OROperatorData) (F : CalConf.BiInput) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : D.toOperator CalConf F U = ∑ I ∈ ORIndex.support D.N, (D.coeffSystem.coeff I : ℝ) * I.toWord.toOperator CalConf F U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For D of type OROperator Data, and I of type ORIndex D.N, I.to Word.expected Output Weight Cal Conf D.input Weights Cal Conf equals D.output weight.
correspondence.leanType: theorem toWord_expectedOutputWeight (D : OROperatorData) (I : ORIndex D.N) : I.toWord.expectedOutputWeight CalConf (D.inputWeights CalConf) = D.outputWeight

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Weight mapping for the OR operator, assuming product homogeneity for words and homogeneity of finite operator sums. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeights (D : OROperatorData) (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (Hprod : CalConf.ProductHomogeneityIdentities) (Hsum : PolyLaplacianCombinationData.OperatorSumHomogeneityIdentities CalConf) : CalConf.MapsWeights (D.toOperator CalConf) (D.inputWeights CalConf) D.outputWeight

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A word-indexed shadow of the OR data. This is useful for reusing the generic Level 8 API, though toOperator above remains the preferred OR-indexed sum. In explicit terms, for d of type oroperator data, its defining expression is N : equals D.N w₁ : equals D.w₁ w₂ : equals D.w₂ support : equals ORIndex.support D.N.image the function sending I to I.to Word coeff : equals the function sending W to ∑ I belongs to ORIndex.support D.N, if I.to Word equals W then D.coeff System.coeff I else 0 support total Power : equals by intro W h W rcases Finset.mem image.mp h W with ⟨I, h I, rfl⟩ exact I.to Word total Power.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{toOvsienkoRedouData}(D)=N = N_{\mathrm{D}} w₁ = w_{\mathrm{D}}₁ w₂ = w_{\mathrm{D}}₂ \operatorname{support} = (\operatorname{ORIndex}.\operatorname{support} N_{\mathrm{D}}).\operatorname{image} (I \longmapsto I.\operatorname{toWord}) \operatorname{coeff} = W \longmapsto \sum I \in \operatorname{ORIndex}.\operatorname{support} N_{\mathrm{D}}, \operatorname{if} I.\operatorname{toWord} = W \operatorname{then} D.\operatorname{coeffSystem}.\operatorname{coeff} I \operatorname{else} 0 \operatorname{support\_totalPower} = \operatorname{by} \operatorname{intro} W \operatorname{hW} \operatorname{rcases} \operatorname{Finset}.\operatorname{mem\_image}.\operatorname{mp} \operatorname{hW} \operatorname{with} ⟨I, _\operatorname{hI}, \operatorname{rfl}⟩ \operatorname{exact} I.\operatorname{toWord\_totalPower}

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
